english: a kicker; of oxen, butting. — rduṅ-ytág (rdung ytag) Lex. w.e., prob. a drubbing, a sound thrashing; rduṅ-ytag byuṅ (rdung ytag byung) I have got a drubbing.

Cs. maimed, mutilated, rdúm-po byéd-pa (rdum po byed pa) to mutilate, lag-rdum (lag rdum) a maimed hand, rkaṅ-rdúm (rkang rdum) a maimed foot, rwa-rdúm (rwa rdum) a mutilated horn; having a maimed hand, foot etc. Mil.

dust, not so much as a deposited mass, but rather as particles floating in the air, motes, atoms; thus esp. rdul-p`ráṅ (rdul phrang), rdul-p`rá-mo (rdul phra mo), rdul-p`ra-ráb (rdul phra rab), nyi-zér-gyi rdul (nyi zer gyi rdul), yet less to express minuteness than infinite number; atom, in a philosophical sense, k`u - k`rág - gi rdul t`ams-čád (khu khrag gi rdul thams cad) all the atoms of the procreative fluid Wdṅ.; monad, rdul-p`ra-rab - ča-med (rdul phra rab ca med), acc. to Was. (279); rdul t`ul (rdul thul), ldaṅ (ldang), dust arises Dzl.; rdul mi t`úl-bar (rdul mi thul bar) (or ma ldaṅ-bar (ma ldang bar)) byéd-pa (byed pa) to lay the dust Dzl.; sprúg-pa (sprug pa), W. *šrúg - čè* (*shrug ce*), to shake off, to beat out; rdúl-du rlóg-pa (rdul du rlog pa) (in this case also t`ál-bar rlóg-pa (thal bar rlog pa)) to crush or pound a thing, until it is reduced to powder Lex.; glaṅ-rdúl (glang rdul) Cs.: 'a mote in the dung of an ox'(?), Sch.: 'a small particle of cow-dung.' — rdo-rǰei rdul (rdo rjei rdul) diamond-powder(?) Lex.; sól-bai rdul (sol bai rdul) coal-dust. Comp. rdúl - čàn (rdul can) dusty. — rdul - p`yágs (rdul phyags) dusting-whisk, dusting-brush Sch. — rdul-tsub (rdul tsub) a whirling cloud of dust. — rdul-tsón (rdul tson) coloured stone-dust, employed in certain ceremonies, for making figures drawn in the sand more visible Mil. nt. — rdul-yzán (rdul yzan) a blouse (?), travelling-cloak against the dust, Wdk. fol. 144 a Lha wears such a garment.
prob. erron. for rtúl-po (rtul po) Dzl. 238, 2.

pf. (b)rdegs ((b)rdegs), fut. brdeg (brdeg), imp. (b)rdég(s) ((b)rdeg(s)), to beat, strike, smite, c. accus., or (less corr.) c. dat., chiefly in B., rdég-čiṅ spyód-pa (rdeg cing spyod pa), verberando concumbere, to compel a wife by blows to fulfil the conjugal duty Thgy.; mé-loṅ-la brdég-čiṅ (me long la brdeg cing) beating the looking-glass in anger Glr.; rdeg-°tsóg-gi sdug-bsṅál (rdeg tsog gi sdug bsngal) the ill-fortune of getting a beating Thgy.; to push, thrust, knock, kick, p`ul-rdég (phul rdeg) a blow with the fist, byéd-pa (byed pa) to give one Mil.; rdeg-čós (rdeg cos) Lex. w.e., Sch. a dance; rdeg - čós - pa (rdeg cos pa) to dance, so perh. Thgy., if brdoy-čós-pa (brdoy cos pa) is not a better reading, ylo-rdég(-tu) (ylo rdeg(-tu)) = glo-búr-du (glo bur du), suddenly.

prob. the original form, but of rare occurrence, for rdáb-pa (rdab pa), pf. brdabs (brdabs), fut. brdab (brdab), 1. to throw down with a clap, to clap the coat-tail on the ground Glr.; with a clashing sound, a potsherd Tar.; to fling or knock down, a person Mil.; lus sá-la (lus sa la) to prostrate one's self, very frq.; rtas (rtas) (to be thrown) by the horse Sch.; *k`a dáb-pa* (*kha dab pa*) 1. C. to fall upon one's face. 2. W. to smack with the tongue, also of the snapping of a spring, of the clapping down of a lid or the cover of a book; *kú-lpags déb-pa* (*ku lpags deb pa*) W. to smack with the lips (in eating). — 2. to throw to and fro, to toss about, mgó-bo rdébs-šiṅ (mgo bo rdebs shing) ¸dré-ldog-pa ('dre ldog pa) to turn one's head this way and that way Pth. — 3. to stumble Sch., so perh. Lt. fol. 196, 6; čal rdáb-pa (cal rdab pa) Lex., rdáb-čal-ba (rdab cal ba) Sch. to slip and stumble. — 4. to kill, to slaughter Bal. — 5. *deb-šóg šé´-pa* (*deb shog she pa*), *táṅ-wa* (*tang wa*) C., *úr deb táṅ-če* (*ur deb tang ce*) W., to talk big, to exaggerate.

dimin. of rdo (rdo), 1. a little stone, pebble, rdeu bskúr-ba bźin (rdeu bskur ba bzhin) like a little stone thrown on the ground Glr. — 2. the stone, calculus, in the bladder or the kidneys, p`o-rdé (pho rde) calculus in males, mo-rdé (mo rde) in females; rdeu čúgs-pa (rdeu cugs pa) the concrescence of a calculus, rdeu ¸dón-pa (rdeu 'don pa) the removing it Cs. — rdel-dkár (rdel dkar) a white pebble, rdel-k`rá (rdel khra) a coloured pebble Cs. — rde-grám (rde gram) ('the spreading of little-stones') the counting with pebbles Cs. — rde-yźál (rde yzhal) a pavement of pebbles. — 3. a musket-ball C., rdeu-pár (rdeu par) a bullet-mould; a bullet-founder C.
B., C., rdó-ba (rdo ba) in W. the usual form, in more recent lit. frq., 1. stone. — 2. weight, for weighing things by a balance,
